records.ety: [OE. oratorie, fr. L. oratorium, fr. oratorius of praying, of an orator: cf. F. oratoire. See Orator, Oral, and cf. Oratorio.]

defs: A place of orisons, or prayer; especially, a chapel or small room set apart for private devotions.
quotes: An oratory [temple] . . . in worship of Dian.
Do not omit thy prayers for want of a good oratory, or place to pray in.

defs: The art of an orator; the art of public speaking in an eloquent or effective manner; the exercise of rhetorical skill in oral discourse; eloquence.
quotes: When a world of men Could not prevail with all their oratory.
